meta.description: Mend Lab is a premier science-driven wellness and physical recovery lab in Cairo, offering advanced recovery protocols, physical rehabilitation, therapeutic massage, cupping therapy (Hijama), and dry needling.

hero.title: Recover Smarter. Perform Better.
hero.subtitle: Mend Lab bridges advanced sports science and traditional therapy to optimize your physical restoration, performance, and overall well-being.

mission.body: To redefine the recovery landscape in Cairo by delivering premium, hygienic, and scientifically backed physical restoration services — while ensuring a comfortable, empowering, and results-driven experience for every client.

services.title: Therapies & Recovery Sessions
services.subtitle: Explore individual treatments or complete recovery packages, each delivered with clinical precision and exceptional hygiene.
services.priceNote: Prices may vary depending on duration and treatment plan.

dry-cupping.name: Dry Cupping (Hijama Jaffa)
dry-cupping.short: Non-invasive suction therapy that lifts tissue to stimulate circulation and release tension.
dry-cupping.description: Dry cupping is a non-invasive therapeutic technique in which suction is applied to the skin and maintained for approximately 5–15 minutes, lifting the superficial tissue to stimulate circulation and tissue mobility.
howItWorks: A vacuum is created inside the cup, lifting the skin and superficial muscle layers.
The lift stimulates local circulation and tissue mobility.
Cups are held in place for approximately 5–15 minutes.
Cups are removed and the treated area is assessed.

wet-cupping.name: Wet Cupping (Hijama)
wet-cupping.short: A two-step clearance treatment combining suction with controlled superficial micro-incisions.
wet-cupping.description: Wet cupping is a therapeutic two-step clearance treatment combining suction with controlled superficial micro-incisions to facilitate the removal of a small amount of blood. It is performed under strict sterile, single-use protocols.
howItWorks: An initial suction is applied to draw blood toward the treatment area.
The cup is removed and superficial epidermal scratches are made with sterile single-use tools.
The cup is reapplied to draw out stagnant capillary blood and interstitial fluid.
The area is cleaned and dressed.

flash-cupping.name: Flash Cupping (Fire / Empty Cupping)
flash-cupping.short: Rapid, repetitive cupping across an area to invigorate circulation and tissue mobility.
flash-cupping.description: Flash cupping involves the rapid and repetitive placement and removal of cups over targeted areas of the body, providing gentle therapeutic stimulation that warms the tissue and boosts circulation.
howItWorks: Suction is created — traditionally using heated air within a glass cup.
The cup is applied briefly, then removed.
The cup is repositioned repeatedly across the treatment area.
The technique is adjusted to your comfort and response.

recovery-massage.name: Recovery Massage
recovery-massage.short: Targeted manual therapy that accelerates recovery after training, load, or repetitive stress.
recovery-massage.description: Recovery massage is a targeted manual therapy designed to accelerate the body's recovery following intense physical activity, high training loads, or repetitive stress. Unlike traditional relaxation massage, it focuses specifically on restoring physical performance and promoting physiological healing.
howItWorks: A brief assessment identifies fatigued and overloaded muscle groups.
Targeted manual techniques address soft tissue, tone, and circulation.
Pressure and pace are tailored to your recovery goals.
The session supports lymphatic drainage and parasympathetic relaxation.

dry-needling.name: Dry Needling
dry-needling.short: Evidence-based needling of trigger points to reduce pain and restore muscle function.
dry-needling.description: Dry needling is an evidence-based technique performed by a qualified physical therapist using sterile, single-use filiform needles to target myofascial trigger points, dysfunctional muscle tissue, and connective tissue. It aims to reduce pain, restore normal muscle function, and improve movement by addressing underlying neuromuscular dysfunction.
howItWorks: A fine, sterile needle is inserted into a myofascial trigger point or affected muscle tissue.
The mechanical stimulation may elicit a localized twitch response.
This helps reduce muscle tension, improve local blood flow, and decrease pain sensitivity.
Unlike acupuncture, it is based on modern anatomy and neurophysiology.
benefits: Reduction of myofascial pain and trigger points
Improved range of motion and flexibility
Accelerated recovery following injury or exercise
Improved local circulation and tissue healing

recovery-sessions.name: Recovery Sessions
recovery-sessions.short: Complete recovery programmes for the upper, lower, or whole body.
recovery-sessions.description: Recovery sessions combine recovery massage, mobilisation, and complementary techniques into a complete, structured programme tailored to a body region. Choose upper body, lower body, or a full whole-body reset — each designed to restore performance and promote physiological healing.
howItWorks: Choose your focus: upper body, lower body, or whole body.
Your therapist builds an evidence-based plan for the chosen region.
A combination of recovery techniques is applied and progressed.
You receive simple guidance to extend the benefits at home.
benefits: Ideal for athletes and active lifestyles

cupping-sessions.name: Cupping Sessions
cupping-sessions.short: Structured cupping programmes for the upper, lower, or whole body.
cupping-sessions.description: Cupping sessions deliver a structured cupping programme across your chosen body region. Blending dry, flash, and where appropriate wet cupping techniques, each session is designed to decompress tissue, boost circulation, and support recovery across the area.
howItWorks: Choose your focus: upper body, lower body, or whole body.
Your therapist selects the appropriate cupping techniques.
Cups are applied systematically across the region.
The session is adjusted to your comfort and response.

about.title: Where sports science meets traditional therapy
about.intro: Mend Lab is a premier science-driven wellness and physical recovery lab based in Cairo, Egypt. Founded to bridge the gap between advanced sports science and traditional therapeutic practices, we deliver evidence-based recovery solutions designed to optimize physical restoration, performance, and overall well-being.

story.title: Our Story
story.body: Mend Lab specializes in advanced recovery protocols, physical rehabilitation, therapeutic massage, and specialized cupping therapy (Hijama) — serving athletes, fitness enthusiasts, and individuals seeking high-quality recovery and wellness services. Every treatment is delivered within a premium, highly hygienic, and professionally managed clinical environment that prioritizes safety, effectiveness, and client comfort.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.body: To redefine the recovery landscape in Cairo by delivering premium, hygienic, and scientifically backed physical restoration services — while ensuring a comfortable, empowering, and results-driven experience for every client.

items.title: Evidence First
items.body: We follow the science, not trends.

items.title: Personal Care
items.body: Programs built around you, not a template.

items.title: Genuine Results
items.body: We measure success by how you feel and move.

leadership.title: Leadership
leadership.name: Ahmed Hosny
leadership.bio: Ahmed Hosny is the CEO and Founder of Mend Lab. With an academic background in Physical Therapy and a strong foundation in sports science and physical rehabilitation, he established Mend Lab with a vision of modernizing traditional therapeutic practices through evidence-based methodologies. His work integrates contemporary sports recovery science with traditional wellness approaches — including Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) and advanced cupping therapy — drawing on extensive knowledge of muscle plasticity, recovery science, performance optimization, and plyometric training mechanisms. Passionate about sports performance and sports marketing, he develops targeted recovery strategies and educational content that help competitive athletes, fitness enthusiasts, and everyday clients achieve optimal physical restoration and performance.
